design_evolution_notes.description: The paper first presents the proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna, then later states that the antenna was simulated and the final patch obtained is shown in Figure 3.
design_evolution_notes.label: Proposal-to-final progression

design_evolution_notes.description: Table I lists the finalized patch dimensions, feed coordinates, and ground dimensions; nearby prose reports close rounded values that appear to describe the same finalized geometry.
design_evolution_notes.label: Final parameter set

design_evolution_notes.description: The conclusion says the antenna can be easily fabricated on substrate material, but no separate fabricated geometry or measured prototype results are provided.
design_evolution_notes.label: Fabrication remark

identified_antennas: Rectangular microstrip antenna reference / introductory example (Figure 1)
Fabrication-ready version mentioned in the conclusion (no separate measured geometry reported)
proposed_final_antenna_rationale: The canonical design should be the final simulated rectangular microstrip patch antenna, because the paper explicitly labels Figure 3 as the 'final patch obtained after simulation' and then reports the realized electrical results against that geometry. Table I supplies the finalized dimensions and feed coordinates, making it the best source for canonical geometry. The proposed Figure 2 antenna and the final Figure 3 antenna are not competing variants; they are the same design before and after simulation refinement. The conclusion’s fabrication remark does not introduce a different geometry or measured prototype, so it is not selected as a separate canonical design.
selected_design_rationale: The paper’s dominant target is the proposed rectangular microstrip patch antenna, and the final simulated version is explicitly identified in the results section as the 'final patch obtained after simulation'. Table I is the clearest finalized parameter source and is more authoritative than nearby prose that reports rounded or slightly reordered values. There are no distinct competing antenna variants; the figure labeled proposed antenna and the final patch figure represent the same design progression rather than separate candidates. The feed is characterized only by its coaxial-probe match location and 50 Ω excitation; no separate structural feed dimensions are explicitly reported, so the canonical feed-dimension list is left empty rather than invented.
selected_design_summary: Dominant design is a simulated, coaxial-probe-fed rectangular microstrip patch antenna on FR-4 for the 2.0–2.5 GHz band, centered at 2.25 GHz. The most authoritative finalized geometry is Table I: patch length 31.43 mm, patch width 40.57 mm, feed coordinates Xf 11.66 mm and Yf 20.29 mm, and finite ground plane 41.19 mm × 50.32 mm.

unresolved_conflicts.description: Table I gives Length 31.43 mm, Width 40.57 mm, Xf 11.66 mm, Yf 20.29 mm, Lg 41.19 mm, Wg 50.32 mm, while prose elsewhere reports approximate values such as 40.1 mm × 31 mm and 11.662 mm × 20.286 mm. These appear to be rounded / reordered descriptions of the same geometry, but the table is preferred as the canonical source.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: Final patch dimensions and feed coordinates

unresolved_conflicts.description: The results section explicitly refers to a simulated final patch, whereas the conclusion notes the antenna can be fabricated. No separate fabricated geometry or measured results are given, so fabrication is treated as contextual rather than a distinct design record.
unresolved_conflicts.topic: Simulation final design versus fabrication mention
